html:
Conch New Material (SZSE:000619): A Bad Screen With a Real Mix-Shift Option

Conch New Material is still priced like a failed PVC and aluminum profile company tied to Chinese property. That is mostly what the historical financials show. It is not what the forward setup is becoming.

The market is right that the company is messy. The 2025 annual report showed revenue down 13.95% to CNY 4.54 billion, a CNY 85.6 million net loss, and a CNY 181.7 million adjusted net loss. The core building-materials segment was still 84% of revenue and only 6.2% gross margin. The stock deserves a dirty headline multiple because there is no trailing earnings multiple.

But that is exactly why the setup is interesting. The stock is not expensive on current assets, and the higher-quality businesses are now large enough to change the consolidated math. At a latest quoted price of about CNY 6.06, the market cap is only CNY 2.67 billion on 441.2 million shares. That is roughly 1.1x Q1 2026 book value, 0.6x sales, and about 7.4x 2025 gross profit, before giving any real credit for the environmental-materials pivot.

The core variant view is simple: investors are looking at the income statement, but the stock is an option on mix. In 2025, environmental materials revenue grew 40.8% to CNY 615 million, with a 19.1% gross margin. Legacy green building materials revenue fell 14.6% to CNY 3.82 billion and earned only 6.2% gross margin. Every CNY 100 million of revenue that shifts from commodity aluminum/PVC into SCR catalysts, catalyst regeneration, environmental engineering, and polymer industrial materials is worth far more than the same revenue in profiles.

This is not the old, overbullish story that the company was magically transformed by a huge asset injection. It was not. The 2024 issuance was 81.17 million shares to Conch Group at CNY 6.16, raising CNY 500 million mainly to repay debt and support working capital. The real transition is more incremental and more investable: Conch New Material is using a repaired balance sheet and the Conch industrial ecosystem to bolt on environmental and functional-materials assets at modest prices.

The most important 2025 deal was the acquisition of 80% of Henan Kangningte Environmental Protection Technology and 100% of Zhengzhou Kangningte Environmental Engineering, aimed at accelerating the SCR denitration catalyst business. The second was the 51% acquisition of Henan Huabei Industrial Plastic, which adds ultra-high-molecular-weight polyethylene pipes, boards, plastic-steel rollers, and conveyor components for cement, steel, coal, mining, and other heavy-industry customers. These are not glamorous businesses, but they fit Conch: low-multiple industrial niches, technical enough to avoid pure commodity pricing, and sellable through a state-backed industrial channel.

The SCR platform is the main upside torque. Conch’s own disclosure says its catalysts cover 160-420°C, include honeycomb and plate products, and are being pushed from power and steel into cement, biomass power, glass, metallurgy, chemicals, nonferrous metals, and waste incineration. The company says its products have been used in more than 2,000 units and 2,200 projects, including 246 cement kiln lines. It also has catalyst recycling and raw-material recovery, which matters because spent SCR catalysts contain recoverable titanium-tungsten and other materials. That gives the business a replacement-cycle element instead of being only new-project capex.

Q1 2026 confirmed the transition is not yet visible in headline net income, but it did show better underlying economics. Revenue was basically flat at CNY 1.026 billion, reported net loss widened slightly to CNY 26.0 million, but adjusted net loss narrowed 16.8% to CNY 28.0 million. Gross profit improved because revenue cost fell faster than sales, while the worse headline loss was partly from lower government subsidies. That is not a clean inflection yet, but it is consistent with a business whose mix is improving underneath a weak demand backdrop.

The cheapness is forward, not trailing. A reasonable 2027-2028 base case is revenue recovering to roughly CNY 5.2-5.5 billion, with environmental and functional materials reaching CNY 1.5-1.7 billion at roughly 20% gross margin and the legacy profile/aluminum/windows base stabilizing at high-single-digit gross margin. That gets consolidated gross margin into the low teens. If operating expenses stay roughly controlled, normalized net profit can move from losses to CNY 180-220 million. At 18-20x forward earnings, fair value is around CNY 8.50-10.00 per share.

The bull case is not just margin repair. It is platform value. Conch Group controls 43.4% of the company and has every reason to use this listing as a small environmental and new-materials vehicle if the first bolt-ons work. More SCR assets, catalyst regeneration, industrial environmental engineering, high-polymer rollers, system windows, and overseas door/window capacity can be added without needing heroic organic growth. A business that today screens as a money-losing building-materials laggard could screen in two years as a state-backed environmental-materials compounder with improving cash conversion.

The downside is real but bounded by asset value unless governance deteriorates. Q1 2026 book value was about CNY 5.63 per share. The company has net debt, thin margins, no dividend, and related-party risk. Related-party structures always deserve a discount because Conch Group can influence procurement, sales channels, asset injections, and capital allocation. But the parent also paid CNY 6.16 per share in the 2024 placement, close to the current price, and its ownership makes abandonment less likely.

The retail context is useful. The stock is still discussed like an old “Conch Profiles” turnaround, with scattered forum attention on buybacks, market-cap management, and theme rumors rather than a serious forward earnings model. The company even had to clarify in February that it had no contact with Musk’s team and that products were not used in aerospace. That is exactly the kind of low-quality attention you want in a small-cap transition: the price has not yet been crowded by institutions underwriting the actual mix shift.

The cleanest way to underwrite it is not to pretend 2026 will be beautiful. It probably will not. The right bet is that losses are the trough optics, environmental materials keep compounding, the Kangningte and Huabei acquisitions contribute a full year, and the legacy business stops getting worse. In that case, the market does not need to pay a premium multiple. It only needs to stop valuing the company like the transition is fake.

Current stock price: CNY 6.06.

Bear case: CNY 4.50, down 26%. Legacy building materials remain structurally weak, SCR growth is not enough to offset fixed costs, acquisitions disappoint, and the stock trades to roughly 0.8x book.

Base case: CNY 9.20, up 52%. Mix shift works, 2027-2028 normalized net profit reaches about CNY 200 million, and the market values the business at roughly 20x forward earnings.

Bull case: CNY 15.00, up 148%. Environmental materials and functional industrial materials become the profit center, Conch uses 000619 as its listed platform, net profit reaches CNY 300-330 million, and the stock rerates to a small-cap A-share environmental industrial multiple.

metadata.company_name: Conch (Anhui) Energy Saving and Environment Protection New Material Co., Ltd. Class A
